title: NAT and PAT Configuration
description: Configure static NAT, dynamic NAT, and PAT (NAT overload) on a Cisco router to translate private addresses to public addresses.
instructions:
## NAT and PAT Configuration Lab

Configure NAT on a border router to allow internal hosts with private IP addresses to reach the Internet.

### Topology

```
[Internal LAN]          [R1]          [ISP]
10.0.0.0/24 --- Gi0/0        Gi0/1 --- 203.0.113.0/30
                10.0.0.1      203.0.113.1
```

### Requirements

#### Part 1: Static NAT
1. Configure a static NAT entry to translate the internal web server (10.0.0.100) to public IP 203.0.113.10

#### Part 2: PAT (NAT Overload)
2. Create an access list (ACL 1) to identify the internal LAN (10.0.0.0/24)
3. Configure PAT to translate all internal hosts to the router's outside interface IP (203.0.113.1)

#### Part 3: Interface Designation
4. Designate Gi0/0 as the NAT inside interface
5. Designate Gi0/1 as the NAT outside interface

#### Verification:
6. What commands show active NAT translations and NAT statistics?
starterCode:
enable
configure terminal
!
! Part 1: Static NAT
!
!
! Part 2: PAT (NAT Overload)
!
!
! Part 3: Interface designation
!
!
end
!
! Part 4: Verification
!
solutionCode:
enable
configure terminal
!
! Part 1: Static NAT for web server
ip nat inside source static 10.0.0.100 203.0.113.10
!
! Part 2: PAT (NAT Overload)
access-list 1 permit 10.0.0.0 0.0.0.255
ip nat inside source list 1 interface GigabitEthernet0/1 overload
!
! Part 3: Interface designation
interface GigabitEthernet0/0
 ip nat inside
!
interface GigabitEthernet0/1
 ip nat outside
!
end
!
! Verification:
show ip nat translations
show ip nat statistics
debug ip nat
expectedOutput:
R1#show ip nat translations
Pro Inside global    Inside local     Outside local    Outside global
--- 203.0.113.10     10.0.0.100       ---              ---
tcp 203.0.113.1:1024 10.0.0.10:1024  198.51.100.1:80  198.51.100.1:80
tcp 203.0.113.1:1025 10.0.0.11:1025  198.51.100.1:80  198.51.100.1:80

R1#show ip nat statistics
Total active translations: 3 (1 static, 2 dynamic)
Outside interfaces: GigabitEthernet0/1
Inside interfaces: GigabitEthernet0/0
Hits: 150  Misses: 2
hints: Static NAT maps one private IP to one public IP permanently: 'ip nat inside source static <local-ip> <global-ip>'
PAT uses 'overload' to allow many internal hosts to share one public IP. The keyword 'interface' tells NAT to use the outside interface's IP: 'ip nat inside source list 1 interface Gi0/1 overload'
Both inside and outside interfaces must be designated with 'ip nat inside' and 'ip nat outside'. NAT will not work if these are missing or reversed.
learningObjectives: Configure static NAT for one-to-one address translation
Configure PAT (NAT overload) to translate many private addresses to a single public address
Properly designate NAT inside and outside interfaces
Verify NAT operation with show commands
